message:
fix(websocket): timeout cancellation-triggered close handshake

WebSocket send() and recv() cancel paths previously called
initiate_close() without a timeout, meaning a misbehaving peer could
block the close handshake indefinitely during cancellation. Both client
and server now wrap initiate_close() in a crate::time::timeout() using
the configured close_timeout, matching the close() method's behavior.

The result of the timeout is intentionally ignored (best-effort close
on cancel) — the Interrupted error is returned regardless.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
